ingredients.name: Otsuki Bridge
ingredients.address: Saruhashi, Saruhashimachi, Otsuki, Yamanashi 409-0614, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Kiruko and Maru cross this traditional wooden arch bridge early in their journey through the ruins of Japan, one of the first recognizable real-world landmarks fans see as the pair head toward Tokyo in episode 1.

ingredients.name: Mansei Bridge
ingredients.address: 1 Chome-1, Chiyoda City, Tokyo 101-0021, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The distinctive red bridge over the Kanda River appears as Kiruko and Maru pass through the overgrown ruins of Akihabara in episode 7, a striking backdrop to their search for the man-eaters.

ingredients.name: Akihabara UDX
ingredients.type: ビジネス センター
categories: ビジネス センター
ingredients.address: 4 Chome-14-1 Sotokanda, Chiyoda City, Tokyo 101-0021, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The UDX building's exterior is faithfully recreated as part of the decaying Akihabara streetscape that Kiruko and Maru navigate in episode 8, the district's familiar tech-tower silhouette now draped in post-apocalyptic quiet.

ingredients.name: Takegawara Bridge
ingredients.address: Takegawara Onsen, 16-23 Motomachi, Beppu, Oita 874-0944, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The historic Takegawara Onsen building in Beppu serves as a location reference in episode 13, its ornate wooden facade evoking the thermal-bath town that the survivors encounter near the end of the series.

ingredients.name: 2 Chome Kobayashidaimonshita
ingredients.address: 2 Chome Shinjuku, Shinjuku City, Tokyo 160-0022, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: A Shinjuku 2-chome street corner appears in episode 10 as a location that Kiruko and Maru pass through in the Tokyo area, part of the dense urban grid of the ruined capital.

ingredients.name: Niiyamadai 2 Park
ingredients.address: Niiyamadai 2 Park, 2 Chome-10-10 Kobayashidaimonshita, Inzai, Chiba 270-1316, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: This small park in Inzai, Chiba is used in episode 10 as a neighborhood location seen in scenes set in the suburbs outside Tokyo, where Maru and Kiruko encounter survivors living in former residential areas.

ingredients.name: Charmant
ingredients.entityContext: Charmant, a small building in the Ueno area, is shown in episode 9 as part of the overgrown urban streetscape that Kiruko and Maru pass through near the Akihabara district.

ingredients.name: Caffè Veloce - Ueno 3-chome
ingredients.localName: カフェ・ベローチェ 上野三丁目店
ingredients.address: Caffè Veloce - Ueno 3-chome, Ｇ－ＳＱＵＡＲＥ上野 １階 3 Chome-17-7 Ueno, Taito City, Tokyo 110-0005, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The Caffe Veloce chain cafe near Ueno appears in episode 9 as a recognizable urban landmark reduced to a crumbling shell, part of the Ueno-Akihabara corridor that Kiruko and Maru traverse.

ingredients.name: Times Sotokanda #12
ingredients.address: Times Sotokanda #12, 3 Chome-11-11 Sotokanda, Chiyoda City, Tokyo 101-0021, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: This parking lot near Akihabara is used in episode 8 as a location Kiruko and Maru move through in the deserted Sotokanda district, its empty bays emphasizing the city's abandonment.

ingredients.name: Oerbierman
ingredients.localName: ウルビアマン 秋葉原
ingredients.address: Oerbierman, 秋葉原ＵＤＸビル １Ｆ 4 Chome-14-1 Sotokanda, Chiyoda City, Tokyo 101-0021, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Oerbierman restaurant in the Akihabara UDX building appears in episode 7 as part of the complex's ground-floor commercial space, its signage intact while the district stands abandoned around Maru and Kiruko.

ingredients.name: Manseibashi Station Remains
ingredients.address: Japan, 〒101-0041 Tokyo, Chiyoda City, Kanda Sudacho, 1 Chome−２５−4 Manseibashi Station Remains, マーチエキュート神田万世橋
ingredients.entityContext: The red-brick ruins of the old Manseibashi rail station, now a heritage retail space beside the Kanda River, appear in the episode 7 Akihabara sequence as Kiruko and Maru pick through the eerily deserted district.

ingredients.name: Akihabara Electric Town
ingredients.address: Akihabara Electric Town, 1 Chome-12 Sotokanda, Chiyoda City, Tokyo 101-0021, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The iconic Akihabara Electric Town streetscape, normally packed with neon and shoppers, is shown as a ghost district in episode 7, the silent storefronts underscoring the scale of the world's collapse as Maru and Kiruko pass through.

ingredients.name: apollostation Manseibashi Self Service Station
ingredients.localName: apollostation セルフ万世橋SS（ヤマヒロ）
ingredients.address: apollostation Manseibashi Self Service Station, 1 Chome-5 Kanda Sudacho, Chiyoda City, Tokyo 101-0041, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: This gas station near Manseibashi appears in episode 7 as part of the Kanda area streetscape that Kiruko and Maru navigate, an everyday landmark now drained and silent.

ingredients.name: Fukushima Bridge
ingredients.localName: 第一只見川橋梁ビューポイント
ingredients.address: First Tadami River Bridge Viewpoint, 天屋原 Kawai, Mishima, Onuma District, Fukushima 969-7402, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The dramatic First Tadami River Bridge viewpoint in Fukushima is referenced in episode 6 as the duo crosses through the mountainous interior of Japan, the sweeping river valley reinforcing the vast emptiness of the post-apocalyptic countryside.

ingredients.name: Night Inn Maki
ingredients.entityContext: Night Inn Maki, a small Tokyo inn near Ueno, appears in episode 6 as a shelter location where characters rest during their journey through the ruined city.

ingredients.name: FamilyMart Nihonbashi Hakozakicho Store
ingredients.localName: ファミリーマート 日本橋箱崎町店
ingredients.address: FamilyMart Nihonbashi Hakozakicho Store, 32-3 Nihonbashihakozakicho, Chuo City, Tokyo 103-0015, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: This FamilyMart near Nihonbashi is used in episode 6 as a convenience store ruin that Kiruko and Maru loot for supplies, a recurring motif in the series of familiar retail landmarks reduced to scavenge sites.

ingredients.name: FamilyMart Kiba 2-chome
ingredients.localName: ファミリーマート 飛島木場二丁目店
ingredients.address: FamilyMart, 2 Chome-９２ Kiba, Tobishima, Ama District, Aichi 490-1444, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: This FamilyMart in Aichi appears in episode 5 as a convenience store ruin that Maru and Kiruko encounter during their travel westward, consistent with the series' motif of gutted chain stores marking humanity's former reach.

ingredients.name: Sensoji Hospital
ingredients.address: Sensoji Hospital, 2 Chome-30-17 Asakusa, Taito City, Tokyo 111-0032, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The real Senso-ji Hospital in Asakusa is referenced in episode 3, its exterior appearing as Maru and Kiruko search for supplies and shelter in the overgrown Asakusa district.

ingredients.name: Dengana Asakusaten
ingredients.localName: 串かつ でんがな 浅草店
ingredients.address: Dengana Asakusa, 椿ビル 1F 1 Chome-9-7 Asakusa, Taito City, Tokyo 111-0032, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The Dengana kushikatsu restaurant storefront in Asakusa appears in episode 3 as part of the entertainment district streetscape that Maru and Kiruko walk through near the temple area.

ingredients.name: Okuyama Gate
ingredients.entityContext: The Okuyama approach gate area near Senso-ji appears in episode 3 as part of the Asakusa pilgrimage that Kiruko and Maru make through the ruined temple district.

ingredients.name: Matsumoto Kiyoshi Asakusa Nitenmon Higashi-sando
ingredients.localName: 薬 マツモトキヨシ 浅草二天門東参道店
ingredients.address: Matsumoto Kiyoshi Asakusa Nitenmon Higashi-sando, 2 Chome-34-3 Asakusa, Taito City, Tokyo 111-0032, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: This Matsumoto Kiyoshi drugstore near Senso-ji's east approach appears in episode 3 as part of the Asakusa shopping street that Kiruko and Maru walk through, its familiar purple signage a touchstone of pre-collapse normalcy.

ingredients.name: Tonkatsu Oribe
ingredients.localName: とんかつ おりべ 浅草店
ingredients.address: Tonkatsu Oribe, 國立ビル 1 Chome-9-1 Asakusa, Taito City, Tokyo 111-0032, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Tonkatsu Oribe restaurant in Asakusa appears in episode 3 as part of the street-level scenery near the Senso-ji area that Kiruko and Maru pass through, its storefront faithfully rendered in the anime's detailed background art.

ingredients.name: Sumida Park
ingredients.address: Sumida Park, 1 Chome-1 Hanakawado, Taito City, Tokyo 111-0033, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Sumida Park along the river in Asakusa provides the backdrop for a scene in episode 3 where Kiruko and Maru rest near the water, the cherry-tree-lined riverbank rendered in the anime's lush, melancholic style.

ingredients.name: Senso-ji Nitemmon
ingredients.address: Senso-ji Nitemmon, 2 Chome-3-1 Asakusa, Taito City, Tokyo 111-0032, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The Nitenmon gate at Senso-ji is seen in episode 3 as Maru and Kiruko explore the abandoned Asakusa temple grounds, a quiet moment of beauty amid the collapsed world around them.

ingredients.name: Otsuki Printing Co.
ingredients.entityContext: A building near Otsuki city appears in episode 1 as Kiruko and Maru navigate the outskirts of the Yamanashi town at the very start of their journey, setting the regional tone of the post-collapse world.

ingredients.name: Otsuki Cosplay
ingredients.address: Japan, 〒401-0013 Yamanashi, Otsuki, 2 Chome−13−16 1F おおつきCosplay
ingredients.entityContext: A street-level Otsuki location near the city center is used in episode 1 as part of the establishing shots of the decayed town that Kiruko and Maru pass through at the opening of their journey.

ingredients.name: Bigecho Otsuki
ingredients.localName: カラオケ ビッグエコー大月店
ingredients.address: Bigecho Otsuki, 1 Chome-2-31 Komahashi, Otsuki, Yamanashi 401-0011, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The Bigecho karaoke building in Otsuki appears in episode 1 as part of the commercial street Kiruko and Maru move through, its familiar chain-store facade now a weathered ruin in the post-apocalypse.

ingredients.name: Otsuki Nursery
ingredients.address: Otsuki Nursery, 2 Chome-10-12 Otsuki, Yamanashi 401-0013, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The Otsuki Nursery building features in episode 1 as a background location in the Otsuki streetscape, part of the residential neighborhood that Kiruko and Maru pass through at the very start of the series.

ingredients.name: 7-Eleven Nihonbashi T-CAT
ingredients.localName: セブン-イレブン 日本橋T-CAT店
ingredients.address: 7-Eleven, 22-1 Nihonbashihakozakicho, Chuo City, Tokyo 103-0015, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The 7-Eleven near Nihonbashi T-CAT terminal appears in episode 6 as another recognizable urban convenience store turned abandoned ruin, part of the Nihonbashi area scenes as Maru and Kiruko travel through central Tokyo.

ingredients.name: Kaminarimon
ingredients.address: 2 Chome-3-1 Asakusa, Taito City, Tokyo 111-0032, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The iconic Thunder Gate of Senso-ji temple in Asakusa appears in episode 3 as Kiruko and Maru move through a deserted Tokyo, the giant red lantern standing unchanged while the city around it has fallen into ruin.
